item.title: These AI startups are growing revenue at faster and faster rates
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: There are a lot of fast-growing AI startups, but some are growing even faster, they say.

item.title: Solo GP Ashley Smith announces second $25M fund to back startups in AI, security and more
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Vermilion Cliffs Ventures announced Wednesday the close of a $25 million Fund II.

item.title: Apple to produce Made in America wireless chips with Broadcom
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Apple has signed a $30 billion+ multiyear deal with Broadcom to design and produce more than 15 billion U.S.-made custom wireless connectivity chips for Apple products.

item.title: Venus Aerospace raises $90M Series B to build a new kind of rocket engine
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Build a new kind of rocket engine and the world will beat a path to your door.

item.title: Blue Origin reportedly raising $10B at $130B valuation
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Blue Origin is raising $10 billion at a $130 billion pre-money valuation from Coatue Asset Management, Bezos himself, and other big-name investors, The New York Times reported.

item.title: Venus Williams-backed WeWard can now lock your apps until you hit your steps
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: With funding from tennis star and angel investor Venus Williams, the French app WeWard says that it increases walking time by almost 25%.

item.title: Former OpenAI exec Kevin Weil is now on the board of Stoke Space
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Kevin Weil's new role at Stoke Space suggests reusable rockets are the next hot thing in Silicon Valley.

item.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

item.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11 billion valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.

item.title: Final extension: Startup Battlefield Australia applications now close July 20
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: If you're building something ambitious, this is a fast track to the people who can move your startup forward.

item.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.

item.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

item.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

item.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

item.title: Google’s Pixel event is set for August 12
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Google's upcoming event in August will introduce new Pixel devices.

item.title: Figma acquires team behind a vibe-coding app
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The Y Combinator-backed company started a vibe-coding platform and later built an agent-creation product.

item.title: Netflix dabbles in shorter video content with its new set of publisher deals with Variety, others
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Netflix is bringing 2- to 20-minute videos to its platform through new partnerships with digital publishers, including Rolling Stone and Variety.

item.title: Hacked, leaked, and held for ransom: The worst breaches of 2026 so far
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: From a massive DOGE data breach and the hacking of critical energy and water systems to the hack of an FBI surveillance system, here are the most damaging security incidents and data breaches of 2026.

item.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

item.title: X adds a video editor to encourage creators to post original content, not stolen reposts
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X is rolling out a new video editor and recorder for iOS with multilingual captions, green-screen effects, and other editing tools.
